summary: Tests cover core library functions (parser, checker, reporter) well with good organization. However, critical gaps exist: (1) CLI integration untested (main function, argument parsing, file I/O errors); (2) Exit code verification missing (claims to exit 1 on denied licenses but never asserts it); (3) Error handling minimally tested (only invalid JSON); (4) Fixtures referenced in workflow test (license-config.json, package.json) not provided in code samples, making integration test brittle with hardcoded output expectations; (5) Requirements.txt parsing mentioned in task but never tested; (6) Act test depends on undocumented fixtures and would fail if they don't match expected structure. Unit tests for parser/checker/reporter are solid, but integration layer is fragile. Would catch regressions in core logic but miss CLI failures, file I/O issues, and workflow problems.
